children.text: I mean totally against one another. Not- and using the Beret, as you know "this is my authority to come in and shoot you, kill you, bomb you, hot wire your car-" and you know, do this kind of stuff. Kidnap your baby, and you know, this kind of stuff that happened. And then Victor got us into some- and I went along with that too. You know, into some kind of trouble, because he busted into Freddie Garcia’s. You know Freddie Garcia from the Victory Temple, did all this work with all these drug addicts and all these ex-alcoholics and he was an evangelist off the- the- the Wilkinson. And we go over there because one of those guys took somebody’s kid and kept her because she wanted a fix or he wanted a- I don't know what the whole thing was, but we went in there. Next thing you know, Juan is all over us 'cause Freddie called Juan and before you know it it was the whole load- "¿Que chingados estan haciendo?" You know? You're- So you know, it got us into trouble big time.

And- and first of all, I didn't WANT to go there. You know, it's just like- this is something that you do. My compadre was there and I just- it was just an incredible thing because, the woman who met me at the door said, “No you didn't come for that, you came because God sent you.” And I'm like "No- I'm here because, you know dah dah dah, dah dah dah." And she goes "yeah..." Years later, and I'll tell you that, because years later I ended up going back, you know, to Freddie Garcia. It's okay if I say that. Que paz de descansa. From the Victory Temple. Somehow that women knew I was going to be there. I was there for a few years. Anyway, so uh, anyway that was kinda like the beginning of the end.

The other thing that happened were the drugs. You know, someone always said that the drugs were the ones that like, the FBI, you know, in planted these- these things and got everybody off on one drug or another, you know. Ramses. Ramsey Muñiz, you know got busted. And you know had so much going for him, you know, it was just incredible. And all these organizations just started, you know, falling one by one by one by one, you know. And the Berets weren't any- you know when Juan said that he was going to hang up his Beret all of said "Well, I'm gonna to hang it up too." I think the only one that stayed was my compadre you know, he stayed with Victor. Because shit, I'm not gonna follow Victor. And I'm not. And I'm still not, you know?

You know, and uh you know there's uh- It wasn't like- you know, I'm not going to do this “me Tarzan, you Jane” shit, no. You know, I'm sorry. I'm not going to do that. I'm not gonna do that for anyone. You know, I don't care who you are, you know? I'm not your servant, and I'm not going to fall behind you either. I'm gonna stand by your side. And that's how I kind of had to prove that to the west side Brown Berets just to get accepted. Because they weren't all in favor of girls, women, coming in either, you know? So it was just like uh-uh. And I'm not- I'm not- And that's the mentality the south siders were coming with. And I am not going to do it, uh-uh. So after that, you know, when Victor took us on that- that- That was it. Don't even ask me to anything for you, ever.

Freddie Garcia was a minister who started Victory Outreach Ministery together with his wife Ninfa in 1970 in San Antonio, Texas. Born in 1938, he was a former drug addict and gang member. After recovering from addiction, he used his faith to help others do the same, opening up his home and later a ministery on San Antonio's west side. He was deeply embeded in his community.

Ramsey Muñiz was a Mexican American politician. He joined the Mexican American Youth Organization (MAYO) while attending Baylor University for law school. As a lawyer working in San Antonio, be became politically active and began traveling as a public speaker. He ran for Governor of Texas twice at the Raza Unida Party candidate, the first Mexican American to do so. Though he did not win, he still recieved hundreds of thousands of votes. Both times, he lost to democratic candidate Dolph Briscoe.
